topic: Sharp partial identification of the average treatment effect from a binary instrument under bounded one-sided defier share and bounded never-taker share: closed-form polytopal identified set, sharpness certificate via boundary distributions, and exact limits recovering Imbens-Angrist LATE point identification when defier share equals zero and Manski-style outcome-support bounds when never-taker share equals one

open_problems.open_problem: Derive closed-form sharp ATE bounds for the binary-IV principal-strata model with simultaneous upper bounds on the defier share and the never-taker share.

source_refs.claim: Gives tight nonparametric ATE bounds for binary outcome, treatment, and instrument using symbolic linear-programming logic, but not simultaneous defier and never-taker inequality caps.
source_refs.author: Balke and Pearl
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Reviews binary-IV ATE partial identification and the Richardson-Robins defier-proportion geometry, leaving cap-based closed forms as a sharper specialization.
source_refs.author: Swanson, Hernan, Miller, Robins, and Richardson
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Shows Balke-Pearl-style IV bounds with covariates are useful but nonsmooth max/min functionals, highlighting the need for explicit facet control.
source_refs.author: Levis, Bonvini, Zeng, Keele, and Kennedy
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.excerpt: include all four observed cells, especially m10(x)=E[Y|Z=1,D=0,X=x]
the formula only uses the Z=0,D=0 mixture and omits the Z=1,D=0 observed cell
What partial-identification bounds remain when monotonicity is relaxed to allow a limited share of defiers?

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published binary-IV bounds either leave the defier and never-taker shares unrestricted, fix a latent defier proportion, or study covariate/inference consequences of existing max-min formulas. The closest prior CausalSmith proposal was rejected because it omitted the Z=1,D=0 observed cell, so it did not solve the full four-cell principal-strata projection with independent caps p_F <= delta and p_N <= nu.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite principal-strata polytope theorem giving lower and upper ATE endpoints as max/min of explicit affine functions of the observed P(Y,D|Z), outcome-support endpoints, the defier cap, and the never-taker cap.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct endpoint-attaining boundary distributions for every exposed facet of the bounded-defier and bounded-never-taker binary-IV ATE polytope.

source_refs.claim: States tightness of the binary-IV bounds; the frontier is certified by feasibility of latent response distributions.
source_refs.author: Balke and Pearl
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Analyzes the binary IV model geometry and bounds under restrictions including small or known defier proportions.
source_refs.author: Richardson and Robins
source_refs.venue: Heuristics, Probability and Causality / University of Washington working paper

source_refs.excerpt: sharpness follows by constructing a two-point conditional distribution
The only delicate boundary cases are p_F=0, p_N=0, and zero observed cell masses
redraft around the full four-cell principal-strata mixture system

open_problems.why_unsolved: LP dual inequalities can certify numerical endpoints, but Stage 1 formalization needs primal latent laws that attain each endpoint. The prior proposal asserted sharpness before its feasible system was corrected, so its boundary constructions do not cover cap-binding, zero-cell, and four-cell mixture degeneracies.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: For each endpoint formula, a constructive map from observables and caps to nonnegative latent probabilities over compliance and outcome-response types attaining the endpoint, together with dual multipliers proving optimality.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove the exact face limits of the bounded-defier and bounded-never-taker envelope: Imbens-Angrist LATE at zero defier share and Manski outcome-support bounds at the pure never-taker face.

source_refs.claim: Identifies the LATE for units induced to change treatment status by the instrument under independence and monotonicity.
source_refs.author: Imbens and Angrist

source_refs.claim: Embeds IV identification in principal strata, including compliers, always-takers, never-takers, and defiers.
source_refs.author: Angrist, Imbens, and Rubin
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Provides nonparametric outcome-support bounds for treatment effects when counterfactual means are not point identified.
source_refs.author: Manski

source_refs.excerpt: then the local average treatment effect E[Y(1) - Y(0) | D(z) != D(w)] is identified
if p_F(x)=0 for every x ... reduce to the usual covariate-adjusted Wald/LATE point estimand
cross-cell means mAB and mBA are latent but constrained by the outcome bounds

open_problems.why_unsolved: The exact-ID LATE and Manski support-bound artifacts are separate from the bounded-defier proposal. A cap-based IV polytope can collapse to a complier-only point estimand on one face and to a support-fill-in interval on another, but no mined artifact proves equality of the projected endpoints at those faces rather than informal consistency checks.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A face-continuity and endpoint-equality theorem identifying the zero-defier face with the Wald/LATE singleton and the p_N = 1 face with the no-assumption bounded-outcome Manski interval.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize exactly when the bounded-defier binary-IV envelope is a singleton, separating the eta = 0 LATE corner from positive-defier slack and never-taker degeneracy cases.

source_refs.excerpt: point identification occurs only through p_F(x)=0, p_N(x)=0, or a singleton hidden-mean projection
The zero-threshold claim fails in the eta=0, pi_N>0 corner
explicitly excluding the eta=0 exact-Wald corner with never-takers

open_problems.why_unsolved: A prior phase-transition statement was rejected because it treated any never-taker mass as fatal to point identification, but at eta = 0 the monotone LATE corner can still identify the complier estimand. The missing obstacle is an iff classification over cap values, observed cell masses, and support endpoints.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An iff theorem for equality of the lower and upper endpoint formulas as a function of defier cap, never-taker cap, observed first-stage cells, and outcome-support degeneracies.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when a never-taker-share cap strictly tightens binary-IV ATE or LATE bounds, and when it is redundant after the observed four-cell constraints are imposed.

source_refs.excerpt: not the difference of two separately optimized marginal intervals unless the joint set is separable
strictness is witnessed by at least one endpoint ... pulled inward by the proper pure-never-taker set
observable sufficient conditions for strict gain, separating coordinate informativeness

open_problems.why_unsolved: The dynamic-IV proposal surfaced pure-never-taker mixture-subtraction geometry, but it was not specialized to the static binary-IV cap problem. A never-taker cap may be redundant if observed D=0 cells already bind it, or strict if the cap removes latent counterfactual completions that otherwise set endpoint outcomes to support extremes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Necessary and sufficient finite inequalities for strict endpoint shrinkage from p_N <= nu, plus witness distributions showing strictness and redundancy regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop a joint sensitivity frontier for ATE or LATE sign conclusions under simultaneous bounds on defier share, never-taker share, and IV independence violations.

source_refs.claim: Parameterizes monotonicity violations by defier share and outcome-distribution differences, deriving sharp bounds and robust regions for LATE conclusions.
source_refs.author: Noack

source_refs.claim: Bounds policy-relevant treatment parameters without monotonicity using the defier-to-complier ratio as a sensitivity parameter, with sharp bounds for binary outcomes.
source_refs.author: Yap
source_refs.venue: Princeton Industrial Relations Section Working Paper

source_refs.claim: Combines defier-share relaxation with c-dependence relaxation of IV independence and derives identified sets and breakdown frontiers for LATE and ATE.
source_refs.author: Picchetti

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing sensitivity analyses usually parameterize defiers relative to compliers or combine defiers with independence violations, but do not include an independent cap on the never-taker share. That cap changes both feasible principal-stratum masses and the width of noncomplier counterfactual outcome components, so the breakdown frontier is not a direct corollary of one-parameter defier bounds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form breakdown surface for sign or magnitude claims in parameters (defier cap, never-taker cap, independence-relaxation radius), with sharp endpoint formulas on each region of the surface.

literature_map:
Manski (1990, AER, Manski1990) supplies the bounded-outcome support-bounds benchmark.
Imbens and Angrist (1994, Econometrica, ImbensAngrist1994) identifies LATE under IV independence and monotonicity.
Angrist, Imbens, and Rubin (1996, JASA, AngristImbensRubin1996) gives the principal-strata language of compliers, never-takers, always-takers, and defiers.
Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA, BalkePearl1997) gives sharp binary-IV ATE bounds through symbolic LP.
Manski and Pepper (2000, Econometrica, ManskiPepper2000) frames monotone instrumental variables and MTR/MTS-style tightening of nonparametric bounds.
Kitagawa (2009/2020, cemmap, Kitagawa2009) studies identification regions under instrument independence and shows when monotonicity adds no ATE information beyond data restrictions.
de Chaisemartin (2017, Quantitative Economics, DeChaisemartin2017) weakens no-defiers to a compliers-defiers condition.
Mourifie and Wan (2017, Review of Economics and Statistics, MourifieWan2017) tests LATE independence and monotonicity through conditional moment inequalities.
Swanson et al. (2018, JASA, SwansonHernanMillerRobinsRichardson2018) reviews binary-IV partial-ID methods and Richardson-Robins defier-proportion geometry.
Noack (2021), Yap (2022), Dahl-Huber-Mellace (2023), and Picchetti (2026) are recent defier/monotonicity-relaxation sensitivity anchors.
Levis et al. (2025, JRSSB, LevisBonviniZengKeeleKennedy2025) shows covariate-assisted Balke-Pearl bounds need nonsmooth-bound inference.
Bai, Huang, Moon, Shaikh, and Vytlacil (2024, NBER, BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024) clarifies which generalized monotonicity restrictions have identifying power for ATE.
Mogstad, Santos, and Torgovitsky (2018, Econometrica, MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018) and related MTE/PRTE work motivate external-validity extensions beyond LATE.
Named tension: the literature has sharp unrestricted IV bounds and exact monotone LATE corners, but not a compact closed-form envelope with simultaneous defier and never-taker share caps plus explicit boundary sharpness certificates.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined doc/formalization/** in parallel with the web scan.
No active proposal tex exists yet for research/pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope; only state and pipeline init files were present.
The closest prior is failed pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance, whose review rejected an omitted-four-cell formula and requested a full four-cell principal-strata mixture system.
That same review flagged the eta = 0 exact-Wald corner with never-takers as a flaw in the old phase-transition claim.
The rejected angle/proposal asserted boundary-distribution sharpness but did not survive the corrected feasible-system review.
Accepted study artifacts imbens_angrist_late_v1 and manski_nonparametric_bounds_v1 provide the two intended limit anchors.
Candidate pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 supplies reusable pure-never-taker mixture-subtraction and strict-tightening language.
The study-pipeline open question oq_defiers independently asks for partial-ID bounds when monotonicity is relaxed to allow a limited share of defiers.
